availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: gsd:quick
availableCommands.description: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:cleanup
availableCommands.description: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:health
availableCommands.description: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:reapply-patches
availableCommands.description: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:complete-milestone
availableCommands.description: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:pause-work
availableCommands.description: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:check-todos
availableCommands.description: List pending todos and select one to work on (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions
availableCommands.description: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-milestone
availableCommands.description: Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:execute-phase
availableCommands.description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-project
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:set-profile
availableCommands.description: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:map-codebase
availableCommands.description: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents (user)

input.hint: [optional: specific area to map, e.g., 'api' or 'auth']

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
availableCommands.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:settings
availableCommands.description: Configure GSD workflow toggles and model profile (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:audit-milestone
availableCommands.description: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:research-phase
availableCommands.description: Research how to implement a phase (standalone - usually use /gsd:plan-phase instead) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:discuss-phase
availableCommands.description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:remove-phase
availableCommands.description: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-phase
availableCommands.description: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:debug
availableCommands.description: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:update
availableCommands.description: Update GSD to latest version with changelog display (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:help
availableCommands.description: Show available GSD commands and usage guide (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:progress
availableCommands.description: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:resume-work
availableCommands.description: Resume work from previous session with full context restoration (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:insert-phase
availableCommands.description: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:join-discord
availableCommands.description: Join the GSD Discord community (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:verify-work
availableCommands.description: Validate built features through conversational UAT (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-todo
availableCommands.description: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-phase
availableCommands.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop (user)

availableCommands.name: observe
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Capture and act on systemic patterns — friction, successes, questions, and ideas. The feedback loop that drives process improvement.

availableCommands.name: help
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Get help with kspec commands and workflows

availableCommands.name: create-workflow
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Formalize repeatable patterns into trackable kspec workflows. Design steps, choose triggers, create loop variants, and integrate with skills.

availableCommands.name: plan
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Translate approved plans into specs and tasks. Import structured documents or create incrementally. Plans persist as durable artifacts with audit trail.

availableCommands.name: reflect
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Structured reflection at the end of work sessions. Identifies learnings, friction points, and improvements for system evolution.

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Kspec-specific review gates — spec alignment, own AC coverage, trait AC coverage, and validation integration. Building block for project-specific review workflows.

availableCommands.name: triage-automation
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Assess and prepare tasks for automation eligibility. Verify spec coverage, acceptance criteria, and task readiness for automated agents.

availableCommands.name: task-work
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Structured task lifecycle — start, work, note, submit, complete. Fix cycle handling, scope management, loop mode for automation, and quality gates.

availableCommands.name: triage
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Triage inbox items systematically. Records decisions with audit trail, then executes actions. Supports inbox, observations, and automation eligibility triage.

availableCommands.name: triage-inbox
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Process inbox items using the record-act pattern. Categorize, promote to spec/task, merge duplicates, defer, or delete stale items with full audit trail.

availableCommands.name: writing-specs
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Create and maintain specification items — modules, features, requirements, acceptance criteria, and traits. The source of truth for what to build.

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show current context usage

availableCommands.name: heapdump
availableCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

availableCommands.name: init
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

availableCommands.name: pr-comments
availableCommands.description: Get comments from a GitHub pull request

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: Review a pull request

availableCommands.name: security-review
availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

availableCommands.name: extra-usage
availableCommands.description: Configure extra usage to keep working when limits are hit

availableCommands.name: insights
availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

Bug audit: tiled layout and drag-drop
────────────────────────────────────────
ULID:      01KKAWKWA9Z47HC5A8PF55ZXJN
Slugs:     bug-layout
Type:      bug
Status:    in_progress
Priority:  2
Automation: eligible

─── Description ───
  Investigate and fix bugs in tiled terminal layout. Check: tile coordinate calculation, drag-drop zone detection, resize handle collision, canvas panning bounds, tile removal orphaning layout entries, split producing zero-size tiles, effective layout reconciliation.
Spec ref:  @tiled-layout
Created:   2026-03-10T03:28:27.465Z
Started:   2026-03-10T03:54:12.373Z

─── Spec Context ───
Tiled Terminal Layout
Type: feature
Implementation: in_progress
Description:
  Flexible grid layout where terminals are positioned as tiles using percentage-based coordinates. Supports splitting, swapping, and multi-tab tiles on a pannable canvas.
Acceptance Criteria:
  [ac-1]
    Given: user is in tiled view mode
    When: a new session is opened
    Then: a new tile is added to the layout with percentage-based coordinates and the terminal renders inside it
  [ac-2]
    Given: multiple tiles exist on the canvas
    When: a tile is split
    Then: the tile divides into two tiles in the specified direction (top/bottom/left/right) each getting half the space

1→export interface TileLayout {

    19→export type DropZoneType =
    20→  | 'swap'
    21→  | 'split-top'
    22→  | 'split-bottom'
    23→  | 'split-left'
    24→  | 'split-right'

    32→interface TileRow {
    33→  y: number
    34→  height: number
    35→  tiles: TileLayout[]
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const MIN_SIZE = 10
    39→const EPSILON = 1
    40→
    41→function tilesOverlap(a: TileLayout, b: TileLayout): boolean {
    42→  const overlapX = a.x < b.x + b.width && a.x + a.width > b.x
    43→  const overlapY = a.y < b.y + b.height && a.y + a.height > b.y
    44→  return overlapX && overlapY
    45→}
    46→
    47→function detectRows(layout: TileLayout[]): TileRow[] {
    48→  const rowMap = new Map<string, TileLayout[]>()
    49→
    50→  for (const tile of layout) {
    51→    const key = `${Math.round(tile.y)}-${Math.round(tile.height)}`
    52→    const existing = rowMap.get(key) || []
    53→    existing.push(tile)
    54→    rowMap.set(key, existing)
    55→  }
    56→
    57→  const rows: TileRow[] = []
    58→  for (const tiles of rowMap.values()) {
    59→    rows.push({
    60→      y: tiles[0].y,
    61→      height: tiles[0].height,
    62→      tiles: tiles.sort((a, b) => a.x - b.x)
    63→    })
    64→  }
    65→
    66→  return rows.sort((a, b) => a.y - b.y)
    67→}
    68→
    69→function findAdjacentTile(layout: TileLayout[], removed: TileLayout): TileLayout | null {
    70→  const rightNeighbor = layout.find(t =>
    71→    Math.abs(t.x - (removed.x + removed.width)) < EPSILON &&
    72→    t.y < removed.y + removed.height + EPSILON &&
    73→    t.y + t.height > removed.y - EPSILON
    74→  )
    75→  if (rightNeighbor) return rightNeighbor
    76→
    77→  const bottomNeighbor = layout.find(t =>
    78→    Math.abs(t.y - (removed.y + removed.height)) < EPSILON &&
    79→    t.x < removed.x + removed.width + EPSILON &&
    80→    t.x + t.width > removed.x - EPSILON
    81→  )
    82→  if (bottomNeighbor) return bottomNeighbor
    83→
    84→  const leftNeighbor = layout.find(t =>
    85→    Math.abs(t.x + t.width - removed.x) < EPSILON &&
    86→    t.y < removed.y + removed.height + EPSILON &&
    87→    t.y + t.height > removed.y - EPSILON
    88→  )
    89→  if (leftNeighbor) return leftNeighbor
    90→
    91→  const topNeighbor = layout.find(t =>
    92→    Math.abs(t.y + t.height - removed.y) < EPSILON &&
    93→    t.x < removed.x + removed.width + EPSILON &&
    94→    t.x + t.width > removed.x - EPSILON
    95→  )
    96→  return topNeighbor || null
    97→}

    99→/** Expand a tile into the removed tile's space along their shared edge only.
   100→ *  Returns null if the expansion would overlap any other remaining tile. */
   101→function expandAlongSharedEdge(
   102→  tile: TileLayout,
   103→  removed: TileLayout,
   104→  remaining: TileLayout[]
   105→): TileLayout | null {
   106→  let candidate: TileLayout | null = null
   107→
   108→  // Check which edge is shared and expand only in that direction
   109→  if (Math.abs(tile.x + tile.width - removed.x) < EPSILON) {
   110→    // tile is to the LEFT of removed → expand width rightward
   111→    candidate = { ...tile, width: tile.width + removed.width }
   112→  } else if (Math.abs(removed.x + removed.width - tile.x) < EPSILON) {
   113→    // tile is to the RIGHT of removed → expand leftward
   114→    candidate = { ...tile, x: removed.x, width: tile.width + removed.width }
   115→  } else if (Math.abs(tile.y + tile.height - removed.y) < EPSILON) {
   116→    // tile is ABOVE removed → expand height downward
   117→    candidate = { ...tile, height: tile.height + removed.height }
   118→  } else if (Math.abs(removed.y + removed.height - tile.y) < EPSILON) {
   119→    // tile is BELOW removed → expand upward
   120→    candidate = { ...tile, y: removed.y, height: tile.height + removed.height }
   121→  }
   122→
   123→  if (!candidate) return null
   124→
   125→  // Verify the expansion doesn't overlap any other tile
   126→  for (const other of remaining) {
   127→    if (other.id === tile.id) continue
   128→    if (tilesOverlap(candidate, other)) return null
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  return candidate
   132→}
   133→
   134→function findOptimalGrid(count: number, containerWidth: number, containerHeight: number): { rows: number; cols: number } {
   135→  if (count <= 0) return { rows: 0, cols: 0 }
   136→  if (count === 1) return { rows: 1, cols: 1 }
   137→
   138→  let bestRows = 1
   139→  let bestCols = count
   140→  let bestDeviation = Infinity
   141→
   142→  for (let rows = 1; rows <= count; rows++) {
   143→    const cols = Math.ceil(count / rows)
   144→    const tileWidth = containerWidth / cols
   145→    const tileHeight = containerHeight / rows
   146→    const tileAspect = tileWidth / tileHeight
   147→    const deviation = Math.abs(Math.log(tileAspect))
   148→
   149→    if (deviation < bestDeviation) {
   150→      bestDeviation = deviation
   151→      bestRows = rows
   152→      bestCols = cols
   153→    }
   154→  }
   155→
   156→  return { rows: bestRows, cols: bestCols }
   157→}
   158→
   159→function makeTile(id: string, x: number, y: number, width: number, height: number, tabIds?: string[], activeTabId?: string): TileLayout {
   160→  const tIds = tabIds || [id]
   161→  return { id, tabIds: tIds, activeTabId: activeTabId || tIds[0], x, y, width, height }
   162→}
   163→
   164→export function generateDefaultLayout(tabs: OpenTab[], containerWidth = 1920, containerHeight = 1080): TileLayout[] {
   165→  // Each tab gets its own tile (no grouping by project)
   166→  const count = tabs.length
   167→  if (count === 0) return []
   168→  if (count === 1) {
   169→    return [makeTile(tabs[0].id, 0, 0, 100, 100)]
   170→  }
   171→
   172→  // For 3+ tiles, lay out in a single horizontal row where each tile gets
   173→  // at least 50% viewport width (canvas extends beyond viewport)
   174→  if (count >= 3) {
   175→    const layout: TileLayout[] = []
   176→    const tileWidth = 50  // Each tile gets 50% of viewport width
   177→    for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {
   178→      layout.push(makeTile(tabs[i].id, i * tileWidth, 0, tileWidth, 100))
   179→    }
   180→    return layout
   181→  }
   182→
   183→  const { rows, cols } = findOptimalGrid(count, containerWidth, containerHeight)
   184→  const layout: TileLayout[] = []
   185→  const colWidth = 100 / cols
   186→  const rowHeight = 100 / rows
   187→  const fullRows = Math.floor(count / cols)
   188→  const lastRowCount = count % cols
   189→
   190→  let tabIndex = 0
   191→
   192→  for (let row = 0; row < fullRows; row++) {
   193→    for (let col = 0; col < cols; col++) {
   194→      layout.push(makeTile(tabs[tabIndex].id, col * colWidth, row * rowHeight, colWidth, rowHeight))
   195→      tabIndex++
   196→    }
   197→  }
   198→
   199→  if (lastRowCount > 0) {
   200→    const lastRowColWidth = 100 / lastRowCount
   201→    for (let col = 0; col < lastRowCount; col++) {
   202→      layout.push(makeTile(tabs[tabIndex].id, col * lastRowColWidth, fullRows * rowHeight, lastRowColWidth, rowHeight))
   203→      tabIndex++
   204→    }
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  return layout
   208→}
   209→
   210→export function validateLayout(layout: TileLayout[], tabs: OpenTab[], containerWidth = 1920, containerHeight = 1080): TileLayout[] {
   211→  const tabIdSet = new Set(tabs.map(t => t.id))
   212→
   213→  // Filter stale tabIds that no longer correspond to actual tabs
   214→  let cleaned = layout.map(tile => {
   215→    const validTabIds = (tile.tabIds || []).filter(id => tabIdSet.has(id))
   216→    if (validTabIds.length === tile.tabIds?.length) return tile
   217→    const activeTabId = validTabIds.includes(tile.activeTabId) ? tile.activeTabId : validTabIds[0]
   218→    return { ...tile, tabIds: validTabIds, activeTabId: activeTabId || tile.activeTabId }
   219→  })
   220→
   221→  // Remove tiles that have no valid tabs and reclaim their space
   222→  let emptyTileIds = cleaned.filter(t => t.tabIds.length === 0).map(t => t.id)
   223→  for (const emptyId of emptyTileIds) {
   224→    cleaned = removeTilePreservingStructure(cleaned, emptyId, tabs, containerWidth, containerHeight)
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  for (const tile of cleaned) {
   228→    if (tile.x < 0 || tile.y < 0 ||
   229→        tile.y + tile.height > 100.5 ||
   230→        tile.width < 5 || tile.height < 5) {
   231→      console.warn('Detected out-of-bounds tile, resetting to default layout', tile)
   232→      return generateDefaultLayout(tabs, containerWidth, containerHeight)
   233→    }
   234→    if (!tile.tabIds || tile.tabIds.length === 0) {
   235→      console.warn('Detected tile with empty tabIds, resetting to default layout', tile)
   236→      return generateDefaultLayout(tabs, containerWidth, containerHeight)
   237→    }
   238→    if (!tile.activeTabId || !tile.tabIds.includes(tile.activeTabId)) {
   239→      console.warn('Detected tile with invalid activeTabId, fixing', tile)
   240→      tile.activeTabId = tile.tabIds[0]
   241→    }
   242→  }
   243→
   244→  for (let i = 0; i < cleaned.length; i++) {
   245→    for (let j = i + 1; j < cleaned.length; j++) {
   246→      if (tilesOverlap(cleaned[i], cleaned[j])) {
   247→        console.warn('Detected overlapping tiles:', cleaned[i], cleaned[j])
   248→      }
   249→    }
   250→  }
   251→  return cleaned
   252→}
   253→
   254→export function removeTilePreservingStructure(
   255→  layout: TileLayout[],
   256→  removedTileId: string,
   257→  tabs: OpenTab[],
   258→  containerWidth: number,
   259→  containerHeight: number
   260→): TileLayout[] {
   261→  const removed = layout.find(t => t.id === removedTileId)
   262→  if (!removed) return layout
   263→
   264→  const remaining = layout.filter(t => t.id !== removedTileId)
   265→  if (remaining.length === 0) return []
   266→
   267→  const sameRow = remaining.filter(t =>
   268→    Math.abs(t.y - removed.y) < EPSILON &&
   269→    Math.abs(t.height - removed.height) < EPSILON
   270→  )
   271→
   272→  const sameColumn = remaining.filter(t =>
   273→    Math.abs(t.x - removed.x) < EPSILON &&
   274→    Math.abs(t.width - removed.width) < EPSILON
   275→  )
   276→
   277→  if (sameRow.length > 0) {
   278→    // Only expand tiles directly adjacent to the removed tile to avoid
   279→    // repositioning distant tiles over non-sameRow tiles (causing overlaps)
   280→    const leftNeighbor = sameRow.find(t => Math.abs(t.x + t.width - removed.x) < EPSILON)
   281→    const rightNeighbor = sameRow.find(t => Math.abs(t.x - (removed.x + removed.width)) < EPSILON)
   282→    const neighbors = [leftNeighbor, rightNeighbor].filter(Boolean) as TileLayout[]
   283→
   284→    if (neighbors.length > 0) {
   285→      const extraWidthPerTile = removed.width / neighbors.length
   286→      return remaining.map(t => {
   287→        if (leftNeighbor && t.id === leftNeighbor.id) {
   288→          return { ...t, width: t.width + extraWidthPerTile }
   289→        }
   290→        if (rightNeighbor && t.id === rightNeighbor.id) {
   291→          return { ...t, x: t.x - extraWidthPerTile, width: t.width + extraWidthPerTile }
   292→        }
   293→        return t
   294→      })
   295→    }
   296→  }
   297→
   298→  if (sameColumn.length > 0) {
   299→    // Only expand tiles directly adjacent to the removed tile
   300→    const topNeighbor = sameColumn.find(t => Math.abs(t.y + t.height - removed.y) < EPSILON)
   301→    const bottomNeighbor = sameColumn.find(t => Math.abs(t.y - (removed.y + removed.height)) < EPSILON)
   302→    const neighbors = [topNeighbor, bottomNeighbor].filter(Boolean) as TileLayout[]
   303→
   304→    if (neighbors.length > 0) {
   305→      const extraHeightPerTile = removed.height / neighbors.length
   306→      return remaining.map(t => {
   307→        if (topNeighbor && t.id === topNeighbor.id) {
   308→          return { ...t, height: t.height + extraHeightPerTile }
   309→        }
   310→        if (bottomNeighbor && t.id === bottomNeighbor.id) {
   311→          return { ...t, y: t.y - extraHeightPerTile, height: t.height + extraHeightPerTile }
   312→        }
   313→        return t
   314→      })
   315→    }
   316→  }
   317→
   318→  // Fallback: find ALL tiles sharing an edge with the removed tile and expand them.
   319→  // Group by which edge they share (left, right, top, bottom of the removed tile).
   320→  const leftOf = remaining.filter(t => Math.abs(t.x + t.width - removed.x) < EPSILON &&
   321→    t.y < removed.y + removed.height - EPSILON && t.y + t.height > removed.y + EPSILON)
   322→  const rightOf = remaining.filter(t => Math.abs(t.x - (removed.x + removed.width)) < EPSILON &&
   323→    t.y < removed.y + removed.height - EPSILON && t.y + t.height > removed.y + EPSILON)
   324→  const above = remaining.filter(t => Math.abs(t.y + t.height - removed.y) < EPSILON &&
   325→    t.x < removed.x + removed.width - EPSILON && t.x + t.width > removed.x + EPSILON)
   326→  const below = remaining.filter(t => Math.abs(t.y - (removed.y + removed.height)) < EPSILON &&
   327→    t.x < removed.x + removed.width - EPSILON && t.x + t.width > removed.x + EPSILON)
   328→
   329→  // Try expanding tiles on one side into the gap. Prefer the side with tiles
   330→  // that together span the full edge (so no gaps remain).
   331→  const tryExpand = (tiles: TileLayout[], dir: 'right' | 'left' | 'down' | 'up'): TileLayout[] | null => {
   332→    if (tiles.length === 0) return null
   333→    const expandedIds = new Set(tiles.map(t => t.id))
   334→    const result = remaining.map(t => {
   335→      if (!expandedIds.has(t.id)) return t
   336→      switch (dir) {
   337→        case 'right': return { ...t, width: t.width + removed.width }
   338→        case 'left': return { ...t, x: removed.x, width: t.width + removed.width }
   339→        case 'down': return { ...t, height: t.height + removed.height }
   340→        case 'up': return { ...t, y: removed.y, height: t.height + removed.height }
   341→      }
   342→    })
   343→    // Verify no overlaps
   344→    for (let i = 0; i < result.length; i++) {
   345→      for (let j = i + 1; j < result.length; j++) {
   346→        if (tilesOverlap(result[i], result[j])) return null
   347→      }
   348→    }
   349→    return result
   350→  }
   351→
   352→  const expanded = tryExpand(leftOf, 'right') || tryExpand(rightOf, 'left') ||
   353→                   tryExpand(above, 'down') || tryExpand(below, 'up')
   354→  if (expanded) return expanded
   355→
   356→  // If nothing else worked, just leave the remaining tiles as-is.
   357→  // A gap is better than resetting the entire layout.
   358→  return remaining
   359→}
   360→
   361→export function splitTile(
   362→  layout: TileLayout[],
   363→  targetTileId: string,
   364→  newTileId: string,
   365→  direction: 'top' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'right'
   366→): TileLayout[] {
   367→  const target = layout.find(t => t.id === targetTileId)
   368→  if (!target) return layout
   369→
   370→  const isHorizontal = direction === 'top' || direction === 'bottom'
   371→  const newLayout = layout.filter(t => t.id !== targetTileId)
   372→
   373→  // The target tile keeps its tabIds; the new tile gets a single tab
   374→  const targetTabIds = target.tabIds || [targetTileId]
   375→  const targetActiveTabId = target.activeTabId || targetTabIds[0]
   376→
   377→  if (isHorizontal) {
   378→    const halfHeight = target.height / 2
   379→    const topTileId = direction === 'top' ? newTileId : targetTileId
   380→    const bottomTileId = direction === 'bottom' ? newTileId : targetTileId
   381→    newLayout.push(
   382→      makeTile(topTileId, target.x, target.y, target.width, halfHeight,
   383→        topTileId === targetTileId ? targetTabIds : [newTileId],
   384→        topTileId === targetTileId ? targetActiveTabId : newTileId),
   385→      makeTile(bottomTileId, target.x, target.y + halfHeight, target.width, halfHeight,
   386→        bottomTileId === targetTileId ? targetTabIds : [newTileId],
   387→        bottomTileId === targetTileId ? targetActiveTabId : newTileId)
   388→    )
   389→  } else {
   390→    const halfWidth = target.width / 2
   391→    const leftTileId = direction === 'left' ? newTileId : targetTileId
   392→    const rightTileId = direction === 'right' ? newTileId : targetTileId
   393→    newLayout.push(
   394→      makeTile(leftTileId, target.x, target.y, halfWidth, target.height,
   395→        leftTileId === targetTileId ? targetTabIds : [newTileId],
   396→        leftTileId === targetTileId ? targetActiveTabId : newTileId),
   397→      makeTile(rightTileId, target.x + halfWidth, target.y, halfWidth, target.height,
   398→        rightTileId === targetTileId ? targetTabIds : [newTileId],
   399→        rightTileId === targetTileId ? targetActiveTabId : newTileId)
   400→    )
   401→  }
   402→
   403→  return newLayout
   404→}
   405→
   406→export function addTileToLayout(
   407→  layout: TileLayout[],
   408→  newTileId: string,
   409→  activeTabId: string | null,
   410→  containerWidth: number,
   411→  containerHeight: number
   412→): TileLayout[] {
   413→  if (layout.length === 0) {
   414→    return [makeTile(newTileId, 0, 0, 100, 100)]
   415→  }
   416→
   417→  // For 1 tile, split it so both tiles stay on-screen
   418→  if (layout.length === 1) {
   419→    const tile = layout[0]
   420→    const tileAspect = (tile.width / 100 * containerWidth) / (tile.height / 100 * containerHeight)
   421→    const direction = tileAspect > 1 ? 'right' : 'bottom'
   422→    return splitTile(layout, tile.id, newTileId, direction)
   423→  }
   424→
   425→  // For 2+ tiles, always append to the right of the rightmost edge.
   426→  // Never split or redistribute existing tiles — that destroys custom layouts.
   427→  const rightmostEdge = Math.max(...layout.map(t => t.x + t.width))
   428→  return [...layout, makeTile(newTileId, rightmostEdge, 0, 50, 100)]
   429→}
   430→
   431→export function computeDropZone(
   432→  layout: TileLayout[],
   433→  draggedTileId: string | null,
   434→  mouseX: number,
   435→  mouseY: number
   436→): DropZone | null {
   437→  // 30% edge threshold makes it easier to trigger split zones
   438→  const EDGE_THRESHOLD = 0.30
   439→
   440→  const targetTile = layout.find(t =>
   441→    t.id !== draggedTileId &&
   442→    mouseX >= t.x && mouseX <= t.x + t.width &&
   443→    mouseY >= t.y && mouseY <= t.y + t.height
   444→  )
   445→
   446→  if (!targetTile) return null
   447→
   448→  const relX = (mouseX - targetTile.x) / targetTile.width
   449→  const relY = (mouseY - targetTile.y) / targetTile.height
   450→
   451→  let type: DropZoneType = 'swap'
   452→  let bounds = { x: targetTile.x, y: targetTile.y, width: targetTile.width, height: targetTile.height }
   453→
   454→  // Calculate distances to each edge (0 = at edge, 0.5 = at center)
   455→  const distToLeft = relX
   456→  const distToRight = 1 - relX
   457→  const distToTop = relY
   458→  const distToBottom = 1 - relY
   459→
   460→  // Find the closest edge
   461→  const minDist = Math.min(distToLeft, distToRight, distToTop, distToBottom)
   462→
   463→  // Only trigger split zone if we're within the threshold
   464→  if (minDist < EDGE_THRESHOLD) {
   465→    if (minDist === distToTop) {
   466→      type = 'split-top'
   467→      bounds = { x: targetTile.x, y: targetTile.y, width: targetTile.width, height: targetTile.height / 2 }
   468→    } else if (minDist === distToBottom) {
   469→      type = 'split-bottom'
   470→      bounds = { x: targetTile.x, y: targetTile.y + targetTile.height / 2, width: targetTile.width, height: targetTile.height / 2 }
   471→    } else if (minDist === distToLeft) {
   472→      type = 'split-left'
   473→      bounds = { x: targetTile.x, y: targetTile.y, width: targetTile.width / 2, height: targetTile.height }
   474→    } else if (minDist === distToRight) {
   475→      type = 'split-right'
   476→      bounds = { x: targetTile.x + targetTile.width / 2, y: targetTile.y, width: targetTile.width / 2, height: targetTile.height }
   477→    }
   478→  }
   479→
   480→  return { type, targetTileId: targetTile.id, bounds }
   481→}
   482→
   483→/** Migrate a legacy tile (without tabIds) to the new format */
   484→export function migrateTile(tile: TileLayout): TileLayout {
   485→  if (tile.tabIds && tile.tabIds.length > 0) return tile
   486→  return { ...tile, tabIds: [tile.id], activeTabId: tile.activeTabId || tile.id }
   487→}
   488→
   489→/** Add a tab to an existing tile's tabIds */
   490→export function addTabToExistingTile(layout: TileLayout[], tileId: string, tabId: string): TileLayout[] {
   491→  return layout.map(tile => {
   492→    if (tile.id === tileId) {
   493→      const tabIds = [...tile.tabIds, tabId]
   494→      return { ...tile, tabIds, activeTabId: tabId }
   495→    }
   496→    return tile
   497→  })
   498→}
   499→
   500→/** Find a tile that contains a tab for the given project */
   501→export function findTileForProject(layout: TileLayout[], tabs: OpenTab[], projectPath: string): TileLayout | undefined {
   502→  return layout.find(tile =>
   503→    tile.tabIds.some(tabId => {
   504→      const tab = tabs.find(t => t.id === tabId)
   505→      return tab && tab.projectPath === projectPath
   506→    })
   507→  )
   508→}
   509→
   510→/** Remove a tab from its tile. If the tile becomes empty, remove the tile and reclaim space. */
   511→export function removeTabFromTile(
   512→  layout: TileLayout[],
   513→  tabId: string,
   514→  tabs: OpenTab[],
   515→  containerWidth: number,
   516→  containerHeight: number
   517→): TileLayout[] {
   518→  const tile = layout.find(t => t.tabIds.includes(tabId))
   519→  if (!tile) return layout
   520→
   521→  const newTabIds = tile.tabIds.filter(id => id !== tabId)
   522→  if (newTabIds.length === 0) {
   523→    // Tile is empty, remove it entirely and reclaim space
   524→    return removeTilePreservingStructure(layout, tile.id, tabs, containerWidth, containerHeight)
   525→  }
   526→
   527→  // Update the tile with the remaining tabs
   528→  const newActiveTabId = tile.activeTabId === tabId ? newTabIds[newTabIds.length - 1] : tile.activeTabId
   529→  return layout.map(t => {
   530→    if (t.id === tile.id) {
   531→      return { ...t, tabIds: newTabIds, activeTabId: newActiveTabId }
   532→    }
   533→    return t
   534→  })
   535→}
   536→
   537→/** Get all tab IDs across all tiles */
   538→export function getAllTabIdsFromLayout(layout: TileLayout[]): Set<string> {
   539→  const ids = new Set<string>()
   540→  for (const tile of layout) {
   541→    for (const tabId of tile.tabIds) {
   542→      ids.add(tabId)
   543→    }
   544→  }
   545→  return ids
   546→}
   547→
   548→export function findTilesOnDivider(
   549→  position: number,
   550→  isVertical: boolean,
   551→  side: 'before' | 'after',
   552→  layout: TileLayout[]
   553→): TileLayout[] {
   554→  const EPSILON = 1
   555→  return layout.filter(tile => {
   556→    if (isVertical) {
   557→      if (side === 'before') {
   558→        return Math.abs(tile.x + tile.width - position) < EPSILON
   559→      } else {
   560→        return Math.abs(tile.x - position) < EPSILON
   561→      }
   562→    } else {
   563→      if (side === 'before') {
   564→        return Math.abs(tile.y + tile.height - position) < EPSILON
   565→      } else {
   566→        return Math.abs(tile.y - position) < EPSILON
   567→      }
   568→    }
   569→  })
   570→}
   571→
   572→/** Find tiles on a divider that form a contiguous segment with the anchor tile.
   573→ *  For horizontal dividers, contiguity is horizontal adjacency.
   574→ *  For vertical dividers, contiguity is vertical adjacency. */
   575→export function findContiguousTilesOnDivider(
   576→  position: number,
   577→  isVertical: boolean,
   578→  side: 'before' | 'after',
   579→  layout: TileLayout[],
   580→  anchorTile: TileLayout
   581→): TileLayout[] {
   582→  const allOnDivider = findTilesOnDivider(position, isVertical, side, layout)
   583→  if (allOnDivider.length <= 1) return allOnDivider
   584→  if (!allOnDivider.find(t => t.id === anchorTile.id)) return allOnDivider
   585→
   586→  const group = new Set<string>([anchorTile.id])
   587→  let changed = true
   588→  while (changed) {
   589→    changed = false
   590→    for (const tile of allOnDivider) {
   591→      if (group.has(tile.id)) continue
   592→      for (const memberId of group) {
   593→        const member = allOnDivider.find(t => t.id === memberId)!
   594→        const adjacent = isVertical
   595→          ? (Math.abs(tile.y + tile.height - member.y) < EPSILON || Math.abs(member.y + member.height - tile.y) < EPSILON)
   596→          : (Math.abs(tile.x + tile.width - member.x) < EPSILON || Math.abs(member.x + member.width - tile.x) < EPSILON)
   597→        if (adjacent) {
   598→          group.add(tile.id)
   599→          changed = true
   600→          break
   601→        }
   602→      }
   603→    }
   604→  }
   605→  return allOnDivider.filter(t => group.has(t.id))
   606→}
   607→
   608→/** Find tiles on the opposite side of a divider that overlap with the given group's perpendicular range */
   609→export function findOverlappingTilesOnDivider(
   610→  position: number,
   611→  isVertical: boolean,
   612→  side: 'before' | 'after',
   613→  layout: TileLayout[],
   614→  groupTiles: TileLayout[]
   615→): TileLayout[] {
   616→  if (groupTiles.length === 0) return []
   617→  const allOnDivider = findTilesOnDivider(position, isVertical, side, layout)
   618→  if (isVertical) {
   619→    const minY = Math.min(...groupTiles.map(t => t.y))
   620→    const maxY = Math.max(...groupTiles.map(t => t.y + t.height))
   621→    return allOnDivider.filter(t => t.y + EPSILON < maxY && t.y + t.height > minY + EPSILON)
   622→  } else {
   623→    const minX = Math.min(...groupTiles.map(t => t.x))
   624→    const maxX = Math.max(...groupTiles.map(t => t.x + t.width))
   625→    return allOnDivider.filter(t => t.x + EPSILON < maxX && t.x + t.width > minX + EPSILON)
   626→  }
   627→}
   628→
   629→

import type { Theme } from '../../themes.js'
import type { Api } from '../../api/types.js'
import type { TileLayout, DropZone, OpenTab } from '../tiled-layout-utils.js'

export interface Project {
  path: string
  name: string
  color?: string
}

export type { OpenTab }

export interface TiledTerminalViewProps {
  tabs: OpenTab[]
  projects: Project[]
  theme: Theme
  focusedTabId?: string | null
  onCloseTab: (id: string) => void
  onFocusTab: (id: string) => void
  layout: TileLayout[]
  onLayoutChange: (layout: TileLayout[]) => void
  onOpenSessionAtPosition?: (projectPath: string, dropZone: DropZone | null, containerSize: { width: number, height: number }, currentLayout?: TileLayout[]) => void
  onAddTab?: (projectPath: string) => void
  onUndoCloseTab?: () => void
  api?: Api
}

export interface TileResizeState {
  tileId: string
  edge: 'right' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'top' | 'top-left' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-right'
  startX: number
  startY: number
  startLayout: TileLayout
  tilesLeftOfRightDivider: TileLayout[]
  tilesRightOfRightDivider: TileLayout[]
  tilesLeftOfLeftDivider: TileLayout[]
  tilesRightOfLeftDivider: TileLayout[]
  tilesAboveBottomDivider: TileLayout[]
  tilesBelowBottomDivider: TileLayout[]
  tilesAboveTopDivider: TileLayout[]
  tilesBelowTopDivider: TileLayout[]
  rightDividerPos: number
  leftDividerPos: number
  bottomDividerPos: number
  topDividerPos: number
}

export type ResizeEdge = TileResizeState['edge']

export type ClientToCanvasPercent = (clientX: number, clientY: number) => { x: number; y: number }

export { TileLayout, DropZone }

1→import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef, MutableRefObject } from 'react'
     2→import type { ClientToCanvasPercent } from './types.js'
     3→
     4→interface UsePanningResult {
     5→  panX: number
     6→  isPanning: boolean
     7→  handlePanStart: (e: React.MouseEvent) => void
     8→  clientToCanvasPercent: ClientToCanvasPercent
     9→  clientToCanvasPercentRef: MutableRefObject<ClientToCanvasPercent>
    10→}
    11→
    12→const DRAG_THRESHOLD = 3 // px of movement before we consider it a drag, not a click
    13→
    14→export function usePanning(
    15→  containerRef: MutableRefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>,
    16→  canvasWidth: number,
    17→  viewportWidth: number,
    18→  viewportHeight: number
    19→): UsePanningResult {
    20→  const [panX, setPanX] = useState(0)
    21→  const [isPanning, setIsPanning] = useState(false)
    22→  const panXRef = useRef(panX)
    23→  panXRef.current = panX
    24→
    25→  const maxPan = Math.max(0, canvasWidth - viewportWidth)
    26→  const maxPanRef = useRef(maxPan)
    27→  maxPanRef.current = maxPan
    28→
    29→  // Clamp panX when bounds change (e.g. tiles removed)
    30→  useEffect(() => {
    31→    if (panX > maxPan) {
    32→      setPanX(maxPan)
    33→    }
    34→  }, [panX, maxPan])
    35→
    36→  const clientToCanvasPercent: ClientToCanvasPercent = useCallback((clientX: number, clientY: number) => {
    37→    const container = containerRef.current
    38→    if (!container || viewportWidth === 0 || viewportHeight === 0) {
    39→      return { x: 0, y: 0 }
    40→    }
    41→    const rect = container.getBoundingClientRect()
    42→    const x = (clientX - rect.left + panXRef.current) / viewportWidth * 100
    43→    const y = (clientY - rect.top) / viewportHeight * 100
    44→    return { x, y }
    45→  }, [containerRef, viewportWidth, viewportHeight])
    46→
    47→  const clientToCanvasPercentRef = useRef(clientToCanvasPercent)
    48→  clientToCanvasPercentRef.current = clientToCanvasPercent
    49→
    50→  const handlePanStart = useCallback((e: React.MouseEvent) => {
    51→    // Middle mouse button only
    52→    if (e.button !== 1) return
    53→
    54→    const startX = e.clientX
    55→    const startY = e.clientY
    56→    const startPanX = panXRef.current
    57→    let dragging = false
    58→    let rafId: number | null = null
    59→
    60→    const handleMove = (moveE: MouseEvent) => {
    61→      const dx = Math.abs(moveE.clientX - startX)
    62→      const dy = Math.abs(moveE.clientY - startY)
    63→
    64→      // Start panning once movement exceeds threshold
    65→      if (!dragging && (dx > DRAG_THRESHOLD || dy > DRAG_THRESHOLD)) {
    66→        dragging = true
    67→        setIsPanning(true)
    68→      }
    69→
    70→      if (!dragging) return
    71→
    72→      if (rafId !== null) return
    73→      rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
    74→        rafId = null
    75→        const panDx = startX - moveE.clientX
    76→        const newPanX = Math.max(0, Math.min(maxPanRef.current, startPanX + panDx))
    77→        setPanX(newPanX)
    78→      })
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    // Suppress middle-click paste when we're dragging (not clicking)
    82→    const suppressPaste = (pasteE: MouseEvent) => {
    83→      if (dragging && pasteE.button === 1) {
    84→        pasteE.preventDefault()
    85→        pasteE.stopPropagation()
    86→      }
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    const handleUp = (upE: MouseEvent) => {
    90→      if (rafId !== null) cancelAnimationFrame(rafId)
    91→
    92→      if (dragging) {
    93→        // We were panning — suppress paste and clean up
    94→        setIsPanning(false)
    95→        upE.preventDefault()
    96→        upE.stopPropagation()
    97→        // Trigger terminal refit after panning
    98→        window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize'))
    99→      }
   100→      // If not dragging, do nothing — let xterm handle paste naturally
   101→
   102→      window.removeEventListener('mousemove', handleMove)
   103→      window.removeEventListener('mouseup', handleUp, true)
   104→      // Defer removal: auxclick fires AFTER mouseup in the same cycle,
   105→      // so the suppressPaste listener must survive until then
   106→      setTimeout(() => {
   107→        window.removeEventListener('click', suppressPaste, true)
   108→        window.removeEventListener('auxclick', suppressPaste, true)
   109→      }, 0)
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    window.addEventListener('mousemove', handleMove)
   113→    window.addEventListener('mouseup', handleUp, true)      // capture phase
   114→    window.addEventListener('click', suppressPaste, true)    // capture phase
   115→    window.addEventListener('auxclick', suppressPaste, true) // capture phase — middle-click fires auxclick
   116→  }, [])
   117→
   118→  return {
   119→    panX,
   120→    isPanning,
   121→    handlePanStart,
   122→    clientToCanvasPercent,
   123→    clientToCanvasPercentRef
   124→  }
   125→}
   126→

1→import { useCallback, MutableRefObject } from 'react'
     2→import type { TileLayout, DropZone } from '../tiled-layout-utils.js'
     3→import {
     4→  computeDropZone,

    10→import type { OpenTab, ClientToCanvasPercent } from './types.js'
    11→
    12→interface DragDropState {
    13→  draggedTile: string | null
    14→  draggedSidebarProject: string | null
    15→  dropTarget: string | null
    16→  currentDropZone: DropZone | null
    17→}
    18→
    19→interface DragDropActions {
    20→  setDraggedTile: (id: string | null) => void
    21→  setDraggedSidebarProject: (id: string | null) => void
    22→  setDropTarget: (id: string | null) => void
    23→  setCurrentDropZone: (zone: DropZone | null) => void
    24→}
    25→
    26→export function useHandleDragStart(
    27→  setDraggedTile: (id: string | null) => void
    28→): (e: React.DragEvent, tileId: string) => void {
    29→  return useCallback((e: React.DragEvent, tileId: string) => {
    30→    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', tileId)
    31→    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move'
    32→    setDraggedTile(tileId)
    33→  }, [setDraggedTile])
    34→}
    35→
    36→export function useHandleContainerDragOver(
    37→  containerRef: MutableRefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>,
    38→  effectiveLayout: TileLayout[],
    39→  state: Pick<DragDropState, 'draggedTile' | 'draggedSidebarProject'>,
    40→  actions: Pick<DragDropActions, 'setDraggedSidebarProject' | 'setDropTarget' | 'setCurrentDropZone'>,
    41→  clientToCanvasPercentRef?: MutableRefObject<ClientToCanvasPercent>
    42→): (e: React.DragEvent) => void {
    43→  const { draggedTile, draggedSidebarProject } = state
    44→  const { setDraggedSidebarProject, setDropTarget, setCurrentDropZone } = actions
    45→
    46→  return useCallback((e: React.DragEvent) => {
    47→    e.preventDefault()
    48→    e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move'
    49→
    50→    if (!containerRef.current) return
    51→
    52→    const isSidebarProjectDrag = e.dataTransfer.types.includes('application/x-sidebar-project')
    53→
    54→    if (isSidebarProjectDrag) {
    55→      if (!draggedSidebarProject) {
    56→        setDraggedSidebarProject('pending')
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      const { x: mouseX, y: mouseY } = clientToCanvasPercentRef?.current
    60→        ? clientToCanvasPercentRef.current(e.clientX, e.clientY)
    61→        : (() => { const rect = containerRef.current!.getBoundingClientRect(); return { x: ((e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 100, y: ((e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 100 } })()
    62→
    63→      const zone = computeDropZone(effectiveLayout, null, mouseX, mouseY)
    64→      setCurrentDropZone(zone)
    65→      setDropTarget(zone?.targetTileId || null)
    66→      return
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    if (!draggedTile) return
    70→
    71→    const { x: mouseX, y: mouseY } = clientToCanvasPercentRef?.current
    72→      ? clientToCanvasPercentRef.current(e.clientX, e.clientY)
    73→      : (() => { const rect = containerRef.current!.getBoundingClientRect(); return { x: ((e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 100, y: ((e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 100 } })()
    74→
    75→    const zone = computeDropZone(effectiveLayout, draggedTile, mouseX, mouseY)
    76→    setCurrentDropZone(zone)
    77→    setDropTarget(zone?.targetTileId || null)
    78→  }, [containerRef, draggedTile, draggedSidebarProject, effectiveLayout, setDraggedSidebarProject, setDropTarget, setCurrentDropZone, clientToCanvasPercentRef])
    79→}
    80→
    81→export function useHandleContainerDragLeave(
    82→  containerRef: MutableRefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>,
    83→  actions: Pick<DragDropActions, 'setDraggedSidebarProject' | 'setDropTarget' | 'setCurrentDropZone'>
    84→): (e: React.DragEvent) => void {
    85→  const { setDraggedSidebarProject, setDropTarget, setCurrentDropZone } = actions
    86→
    87→  return useCallback((e: React.DragEvent) => {
    88→    const rect = containerRef.current?.getBoundingClientRect()
    89→    if (rect) {
    90→      const { clientX, clientY } = e
    91→      if (clientX < rect.left || clientX > rect.right || clientY < rect.top || clientY > rect.bottom) {
    92→        setCurrentDropZone(null)
    93→        setDropTarget(null)
    94→        setDraggedSidebarProject(null)
    95→      }
    96→    }
    97→  }, [containerRef, setDraggedSidebarProject, setDropTarget, setCurrentDropZone])
    98→}
    99→
   100→export function useApplyDropZone(
   101→  tabs: OpenTab[],
   102→  containerSizeRef: MutableRefObject<{ width: number; height: number }>
   103→): (layout: TileLayout[], draggedId: string, zone: DropZone) => TileLayout[] {
   104→  return useCallback((layout: TileLayout[], draggedId: string, zone: DropZone): TileLayout[] => {
   105→    const { width, height } = containerSizeRef.current
   106→
   107→    if (zone.type === 'swap') {
   108→      const sourceTile = layout.find(t => t.id === draggedId)
   109→      const targetTile = layout.find(t => t.id === zone.targetTileId)
   110→
   111→      if (sourceTile && targetTile) {
   112→        // Check if source and target share a project → merge tabs
   113→        const sourceProject = sourceTile.tabIds
   114→          .map(id => tabs.find(t => t.id === id)?.projectPath)
   115→          .find(p => p != null)
   116→        const targetProject = targetTile.tabIds
   117→          .map(id => tabs.find(t => t.id === id)?.projectPath)
   118→          .find(p => p != null)
   119→
   120→        if (sourceProject && sourceProject === targetProject) {
   121→          // Merge: add source tabs into target tile, then remove source tile
   122→          let merged = layout
   123→          for (const tabId of sourceTile.tabIds) {
   124→            merged = addTabToExistingTile(merged, targetTile.id, tabId)
   125→          }
   126→          return removeTilePreservingStructure(merged, draggedId, tabs, width, height)
   127→        }
   128→
   129→        // Different projects → swap positions
   130→        return layout.map(tile => {
   131→          if (tile.id === draggedId) {
   132→            return { ...tile, x: targetTile.x, y: targetTile.y, width: targetTile.width, height: targetTile.height }
   133→          }
   134→          if (tile.id === zone.targetTileId) {
   135→            return { ...tile, x: sourceTile.x, y: sourceTile.y, width: sourceTile.width, height: sourceTile.height }
   136→          }
   137→          return tile
   138→        })
   139→      }
   140→      return layout
   141→    }
   142→
   143→    const direction = zone.type.replace('split-', '') as 'top' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'right'
   144→    // For drag operations, just remove the tile without expanding neighbors.
   145→    // The tile is being moved, not deleted — expansion would distort the layout.
   146→    const withoutDragged = layout.filter(t => t.id !== draggedId)
   147→    return splitTile(withoutDragged, zone.targetTileId, draggedId, direction)
   148→  }, [tabs, containerSizeRef])
   149→}

     1→import { useCallback, MutableRefObject } from 'react'
     2→import type { TileLayout, DropZone } from '../tiled-layout-utils.js'
     3→import {
     4→  computeDropZone,
     5→  validateLayout,
     6→  removeTilePreservingStructure,
     7→  splitTile,
     8→  addTabToExistingTile
     9→} from '../tiled-layout-utils.js'
    10→import type { OpenTab, ClientToCanvasPercent } from './types.js'
    11→
    12→interface DragDropState {
    13→  draggedTile: string | null
    14→  draggedSidebarProject: string | null
    15→  dropTarget: string | null
    16→  currentDropZone: DropZone | null
    17→}
    18→
    19→interface DragDropActions {
    20→  setDraggedTile: (id: string | null) => void
    21→  setDraggedSidebarProject: (id: string | null) => void
    22→  setDropTarget: (id: string | null) => void
    23→  setCurrentDropZone: (zone: DropZone | null) => void
    24→}
    25→
    26→export function useHandleDragStart(
    27→  setDraggedTile: (id: string | null) => void
    28→): (e: React.DragEvent, tileId: string) => void {
    29→  return useCallback((e: React.DragEvent, tileId: string) => {
    30→    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', tileId)
    31→    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move'
    32→    setDraggedTile(tileId)
    33→  }, [setDraggedTile])
    34→}
    35→
    36→export function useHandleContainerDragOver(
    37→  containerRef: MutableRefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>,
    38→  effectiveLayout: TileLayout[],
    39→  state: Pick<DragDropState, 'draggedTile' | 'draggedSidebarProject'>,
    40→  actions: Pick<DragDropActions, 'setDraggedSidebarProject' | 'setDropTarget' | 'setCurrentDropZone'>,
    41→  clientToCanvasPercentRef?: MutableRefObject<ClientToCanvasPercent>
    42→): (e: React.DragEvent) => void {
    43→  const { draggedTile, draggedSidebarProject } = state
    44→  const { setDraggedSidebarProject, setDropTarget, setCurrentDropZone } = actions
    45→
    46→  return useCallback((e: React.DragEvent) => {
    47→    e.preventDefault()
    48→    e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move'
    49→
    50→    if (!containerRef.current) return
    51→
    52→    const isSidebarProjectDrag = e.dataTransfer.types.includes('application/x-sidebar-project')
    53→
    54→    if (isSidebarProjectDrag) {
    55→      if (!draggedSidebarProject) {
    56→        setDraggedSidebarProject('pending')
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      const { x: mouseX, y: mouseY } = clientToCanvasPercentRef?.current
    60→        ? clientToCanvasPercentRef.current(e.clientX, e.clientY)
    61→        : (() => { const rect = containerRef.current!.getBoundingClientRect(); return { x: ((e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 100, y: ((e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 100 } })()
    62→
    63→      const zone = computeDropZone(effectiveLayout, null, mouseX, mouseY)
    64→      setCurrentDropZone(zone)
    65→      setDropTarget(zone?.targetTileId || null)
    66→      return
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    if (!draggedTile) return
    70→
    71→    const { x: mouseX, y: mouseY } = clientToCanvasPercentRef?.current
    72→      ? clientToCanvasPercentRef.current(e.clientX, e.clientY)
    73→      : (() => { const rect = containerRef.current!.getBoundingClientRect(); return { x: ((e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 100, y: ((e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 100 } })()
    74→
    75→    const zone = computeDropZone(effectiveLayout, draggedTile, mouseX, mouseY)
    76→    setCurrentDropZone(zone)
    77→    setDropTarget(zone?.targetTileId || null)
    78→  }, [containerRef, draggedTile, draggedSidebarProject, effectiveLayout, setDraggedSidebarProject, setDropTarget, setCurrentDropZone, clientToCanvasPercentRef])
    79→}
    80→
    81→export function useHandleContainerDragLeave(
    82→  containerRef: MutableRefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>,
    83→  actions: Pick<DragDropActions, 'setDraggedSidebarProject' | 'setDropTarget' | 'setCurrentDropZone'>
    84→): (e: React.DragEvent) => void {
    85→  const { setDraggedSidebarProject, setDropTarget, setCurrentDropZone } = actions
    86→
    87→  return useCallback((e: React.DragEvent) => {
    88→    const rect = containerRef.current?.getBoundingClientRect()
    89→    if (rect) {
    90→      const { clientX, clientY } = e
    91→      if (clientX < rect.left || clientX > rect.right || clientY < rect.top || clientY > rect.bottom) {
    92→        setCurrentDropZone(null)
    93→        setDropTarget(null)
    94→        setDraggedSidebarProject(null)
    95→      }
    96→    }
    97→  }, [containerRef, setDraggedSidebarProject, setDropTarget, setCurrentDropZone])
    98→}
    99→
   100→export function useApplyDropZone(
   101→  tabs: OpenTab[],
   102→  containerSizeRef: MutableRefObject<{ width: number; height: number }>
   103→): (layout: TileLayout[], draggedId: string, zone: DropZone) => TileLayout[] {
   104→  return useCallback((layout: TileLayout[], draggedId: string, zone: DropZone): TileLayout[] => {
   105→    const { width, height } = containerSizeRef.current
   106→
   107→    if (zone.type === 'swap') {

   110→

   112→        // Check if source and target share a project → merge tabs
   113→        const sourceProject = sourceTile.tabIds
   114→          .map(id => tabs.find(t => t.id === id)?.projectPath)
   115→          .find(p => p != null)

   120→        if (sourceProject && sourceProject === targetProject) {
   121→          // Merge: add source tabs into target tile, then remove source tile
   122→          let merged = layout

   124→            merged = addTabToExistingTile(merged, targetTile.id, tabId)

   129→        // Different projects → swap positions
   130→        return layout.map(tile => {
   131→          if (tile.id === draggedId) {

   133→          }

     5→
     6→function getCursorForEdge(edge: string): string {

     8→    case 'right':
     9→    case 'left':

    11→    case 'top':
    12→    case 'bottom':
    13→      return 'ns-resize'
    14→    case 'top-left':
    15→    case 'bottom-right':
    16→      return 'nwse-resize'
    17→    case 'top-right':

    24→
    25→export function useStartTileResize(
    26→  effectiveLayout: TileLayout[],
    27→  setTileResizing: (state: TileResizeState | null) => void,
    28→  setHoveredEdge: (state: { tileId: string; edge: string } | null) => void
    29→): (e: React.MouseEvent, tileId: string, edge: ResizeEdge) => void {
    30→  return useCallback((e: React.MouseEvent, tileId: string, edge: ResizeEdge) => {
    31→    e.preventDefault()
    32→    e.stopPropagation()
    33→    setHoveredEdge(null)
    34→    const tile = effectiveLayout.find(t => t.id === tileId)
    35→    if (tile) {
    36→      const rightDivider = tile.x + tile.width
    37→      const leftDivider = tile.x
    38→      const bottomDivider = tile.y + tile.height
    39→      const topDivider = tile.y
    40→
    41→      // Find contiguous tile groups on each divider (only tiles connected to this tile)
    42→      // then find overlapping tiles on the opposite side of each divider
    43→      const tilesLeftOfRight = findContiguousTilesOnDivider(rightDivider, true, 'before', effectiveLayout, tile)
    44→      const tilesRightOfRight = findOverlappingTilesOnDivider(rightDivider, true, 'after', effectiveLayout, tilesLeftOfRight)
    45→      const tilesRightOfLeft = findContiguousTilesOnDivider(leftDivider, true, 'after', effectiveLayout, tile)
    46→      const tilesLeftOfLeft = findOverlappingTilesOnDivider(leftDivider, true, 'before', effectiveLayout, tilesRightOfLeft)
    47→      const tilesAboveBottom = findContiguousTilesOnDivider(bottomDivider, false, 'before', effectiveLayout, tile)
    48→      const tilesBelowBottom = findOverlappingTilesOnDivider(bottomDivider, false, 'after', effectiveLayout, tilesAboveBottom)
    49→      const tilesBelowTop = findContiguousTilesOnDivider(topDivider, false, 'after', effectiveLayout, tile)
    50→      const tilesAboveTop = findOverlappingTilesOnDivider(topDivider, false, 'before', effectiveLayout, tilesBelowTop)
    51→
    52→      setTileResizing({
    53→        tileId,
    54→        edge,
    55→        startX: e.clientX,
    56→        startY: e.clientY,
    57→        startLayout: { ...tile },
    58→        tilesLeftOfRightDivider: tilesLeftOfRight,
    59→        tilesRightOfRightDivider: tilesRightOfRight,
    60→        tilesLeftOfLeftDivider: tilesLeftOfLeft,
    61→        tilesRightOfLeftDivider: tilesRightOfLeft,
    62→        tilesAboveBottomDivider: tilesAboveBottom,
    63→        tilesBelowBottomDivider: tilesBelowBottom,
    64→        tilesAboveTopDivider: tilesAboveTop,
    65→        tilesBelowTopDivider: tilesBelowTop,
    66→        rightDividerPos: rightDivider,
    67→        leftDividerPos: leftDivider,
    68→        bottomDividerPos: bottomDivider,
    69→        topDividerPos: topDivider
    70→      })
    71→    }
    72→  }, [effectiveLayout, setTileResizing, setHoveredEdge])
    73→}
    74→
    75→export function useTileResizeEffect(
    76→  tileResizing: TileResizeState | null,
    77→  containerRef: MutableRefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>,
    78→  effectiveLayoutRef: MutableRefObject<TileLayout[]>,
    79→  onLayoutChangeRef: MutableRefObject<(layout: TileLayout[]) => void>,
    80→  setTileResizing: (state: TileResizeState | null) => void,
    81→  clientToCanvasPercentRef?: MutableRefObject<ClientToCanvasPercent>
    82→): void {
    83→  useEffect(() => {
    84→    if (!tileResizing || !containerRef.current) return
    85→
    86→    let rafId: number | null = null
    87→    const handleTileResizeMove = (e: MouseEvent) => {
    88→      if (!containerRef.current) return
    89→      if (rafId !== null) return
    90→      rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
    91→        rafId = null
    92→        if (!containerRef.current) return
    93→        const rect = containerRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()
    94→        const {
    95→          edge,
    96→          tilesLeftOfRightDivider, tilesRightOfRightDivider,
    97→          tilesLeftOfLeftDivider, tilesRightOfLeftDivider,
    98→          tilesAboveBottomDivider, tilesBelowBottomDivider,
    99→          tilesAboveTopDivider, tilesBelowTopDivider,
   100→          rightDividerPos, leftDividerPos, bottomDividerPos, topDividerPos
   101→        } = tileResizing
   102→        const currentLayout = effectiveLayoutRef.current
   103→
   104→        const canvasCoords = clientToCanvasPercentRef?.current
   105→          ? clientToCanvasPercentRef.current(e.clientX, e.clientY)
   106→          : { x: ((e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 100, y: ((e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 100 }
   107→        const mouseXPercent = canvasCoords.x
   108→        const mouseYPercent = canvasCoords.y
   109→
   110→        const tileUpdates = new Map<string, TileLayout>()
   111→
   112→        const moveVerticalDivider = (originalPos: number, tilesLeft: TileLayout[], tilesRight: TileLayout[]) => {
   113→          if (tilesLeft.length === 0 && tilesRight.length === 0) return
   114→          // Block left outer boundary (x=0) but allow free resize otherwise
   115→          if (tilesLeft.length === 0 && originalPos < 1) return
   116→          let minPos = 0
   117→          let maxPos = Infinity
   118→          tilesLeft.forEach(tile => { minPos = Math.max(minPos, tile.x + MIN_SIZE) })
   119→          tilesRight.forEach(tile => { maxPos = Math.min(maxPos, tile.x + tile.width - MIN_SIZE) })
   120→          const newPos = Math.max(minPos, Math.min(maxPos, mouseXPercent))
   121→
   122→          tilesLeft.forEach(tile => {
   123→            const currentTile = currentLayout.find(t => t.id === tile.id) || tile
   124→            const existing = tileUpdates.get(tile.id) || { ...currentTile }
   125→            existing.width = newPos - tile.x
   126→            tileUpdates.set(tile.id, existing)
   127→          })
   128→          tilesRight.forEach(tile => {
   129→            const currentTile = currentLayout.find(t => t.id === tile.id) || tile
   130→            const existing = tileUpdates.get(tile.id) || { ...currentTile }
   131→            const originalRight = tile.x + tile.width
   132→            existing.x = newPos
   133→            existing.width = originalRight - newPos
   134→            tileUpdates.set(tile.id, existing)
   135→          })
   136→        }
   137→
   138→        const moveHorizontalDivider = (originalPos: number, tilesAbove: TileLayout[], tilesBelow: TileLayout[]) => {
   139→          if (tilesAbove.length === 0 && tilesBelow.length === 0) return
   140→          let minPos = 0
   141→          let maxPos = 100

   143→          tilesBelow.forEach(tile => { maxPos = Math.min(maxPos, tile.y + tile.height - MIN_SIZE) })

   125→            existing.width = newPos - tile.x
   126→            tileUpdates.set(tile.id, existing)
   127→          })
   128→          tilesRight.forEach(tile => {
   129→            const currentTile = currentLayout.find(t => t.id === tile.id) || tile
   130→            const existing = tileUpdates.get(tile.id) || { ...currentTile }
   131→            const originalRight = tile.x + tile.width
   132→            existing.x = newPos
   133→            existing.width = originalRight - newPos
   134→            tileUpdates.set(tile.id, existing)
   135→          })
   136→        }
   137→
   138→        const moveHorizontalDivider = (originalPos: number, tilesAbove: TileLayout[], tilesBelow: TileLayout[]) => {
   139→          if (tilesAbove.length === 0 && tilesBelow.length === 0) return
   140→          let minPos = 0
   141→          let maxPos = 100
   142→          tilesAbove.forEach(tile => { minPos = Math.max(minPos, tile.y + MIN_SIZE) })
   143→          tilesBelow.forEach(tile => { maxPos = Math.min(maxPos, tile.y + tile.height - MIN_SIZE) })
   144→          const newPos = Math.max(minPos, Math.min(maxPos, mouseYPercent))
   145→
   146→          tilesAbove.forEach(tile => {
   147→            const currentTile = currentLayout.find(t => t.id === tile.id) || tile
   148→            const existing = tileUpdates.get(tile.id) || { ...currentTile }
   149→            existing.height = newPos - tile.y
   150→            tileUpdates.set(tile.id, existing)

1→import { useMemo, useEffect, useRef, MutableRefObject } from 'react'
     2→import type { TileLayout } from '../tiled-layout-utils.js'
     3→import {
     4→  generateDefaultLayout,
     5→  validateLayout,
     6→  addTileToLayout,
     7→  removeTabFromTile,
     8→  getAllTabIdsFromLayout,
     9→  migrateTile
    10→} from '../tiled-layout-utils.js'
    11→import type { OpenTab } from './types.js'
    12→
    13→export function useEffectiveLayout(
    14→  layout: TileLayout[],
    15→  tabs: OpenTab[],
    16→  containerSizeRef: MutableRefObject<{ width: number; height: number }>,
    17→  onLayoutChange: (layout: TileLayout[]) => void
    18→): {
    19→  effectiveLayout: TileLayout[]
    20→  effectiveLayoutRef: MutableRefObject<TileLayout[]>
    21→  activeTabIdRef: MutableRefObject<string | null>
    22→} {
    23→  const effectiveLayoutRef = useRef<TileLayout[]>([])
    24→  const activeTabIdRef = useRef<string | null>(tabs.length > 0 ? tabs[tabs.length - 1].id : null)
    25→
    26→  const effectiveLayout = useMemo(() => {
    27→    const { width, height } = containerSizeRef.current
    28→
    29→    if (layout.length === 0) {
    30→      return generateDefaultLayout(tabs, width, height)
    31→    }
    32→
    33→    // Ensure all tiles are migrated to the new format
    34→    const migratedLayout = layout.map(migrateTile)
    35→
    36→    const layoutTabIds = getAllTabIdsFromLayout(migratedLayout)
    37→    const tabIds = new Set(tabs.map(t => t.id))
    38→    const addedTabs = tabs.filter(t => !layoutTabIds.has(t.id))
    39→    const removedTabIds = [...layoutTabIds].filter(id => !tabIds.has(id))
    40→
    41→    if (addedTabs.length === 0 && removedTabIds.length === 0) {
    42→      return validateLayout(migratedLayout, tabs, width, height)
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    let newLayout = [...migratedLayout]
    46→
    47→    // Remove tabs that no longer exist
    48→    for (const removedId of removedTabIds) {
    49→      newLayout = removeTabFromTile(newLayout, removedId, tabs, width, height)
    50→    }
    51→
    52→    // Add new tabs - each gets its own tile (no auto-grouping by project)
    53→    for (const addedTab of addedTabs) {
    54→      const existingIds = newLayout.map(l => l.id)
    55→      const activeId = existingIds.length > 0 ? existingIds[existingIds.length - 1] : null
    56→      newLayout = addTileToLayout(newLayout, addedTab.id, activeId, width, height)
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    if (tabs.length > 0) {
    60→      activeTabIdRef.current = tabs[tabs.length - 1].id
    61→    }
    62→
    63→    return validateLayout(newLayout, tabs, width, height)
    64→  }, [layout, tabs, containerSizeRef])
    65→
    66→  effectiveLayoutRef.current = effectiveLayout
    67→
    68→  useEffect(() => {
    69→    const layoutTabIds = getAllTabIdsFromLayout(layout.map(migrateTile))
    70→    const effectiveTabIds = getAllTabIdsFromLayout(effectiveLayout)
    71→    const idsMatch = layoutTabIds.size === effectiveTabIds.size &&
    72→      [...layoutTabIds].every(id => effectiveTabIds.has(id))
    73→
    74→    if (!idsMatch) {
    75→      onLayoutChange(effectiveLayout)
    76→    }
    77→  }, [effectiveLayout, layout, onLayoutChange])
    78→
    79→  return { effectiveLayout, effectiveLayoutRef, activeTabIdRef }
    80→}
    81→

import React, { useCallback } from 'react'
import { Terminal } from '../Terminal.js'
import { ErrorBoundary } from '../ErrorBoundary.js'
import type { TileLayout, DropZone } from '../tiled-layout-utils.js'
import { computeDropZone } from '../tiled-layout-utils.js'
import type { Theme } from '../../themes.js'
import type { Api } from '../../api/types.js'
import type { OpenTab, Project, ResizeEdge, ClientToCanvasPercent } from './types.js'

interface TileTerminalProps {
  tile: TileLayout
  tabs: OpenTab[]
  activeSubTabId: string
  project: Project | undefined
  theme: Theme
  api: Api | undefined
  GAP: number
  isFocused: boolean
  isDragging: boolean
  isDropTarget: boolean
  draggedTile: string | null
  draggedSidebarProject: string | null
  effectiveLayout: TileLayout[]
  containerRef: React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>
  highlightedEdges: Set<string>
  viewportSize: { width: number; height: number }
  clientToCanvasPercent: ClientToCanvasPercent
  onCloseTab: (id: string) => void
  onFocusTab: (id: string) => void
  onSwitchSubTab: (tileId: string, tabId: string) => void
  onAddTab?: (projectPath: string) => void
  onDragStart: (e: React.DragEvent, tileId: string) => void
  onDragEnd: () => void
  onContainerDrop: (e: React.DragEvent) => void
  startTileResize: (e: React.MouseEvent, tileId: string, edge: ResizeEdge) => void
  setHoveredEdge: (state: { tileId: string; edge: string } | null) => void
  setDraggedSidebarProject: (id: string | null) => void
  setCurrentDropZone: (zone: DropZone | null) => void
  setDropTarget: (id: string | null) => void
}

export function TileTerminal({
  tile,
  tabs,
  activeSubTabId,
  project,
  theme,
  api,
  GAP,
  isFocused,
  isDragging,
  isDropTarget,
  draggedTile,
  draggedSidebarProject,
  effectiveLayout,
  containerRef,
  highlightedEdges,
  viewportSize,
  clientToCanvasPercent,
  onCloseTab,
  onFocusTab,
  onSwitchSubTab,
  onAddTab,
  onDragStart,
  onDragEnd,
  onContainerDrop,
  startTileResize,
  setHoveredEdge,
  setDraggedSidebarProject,
  setCurrentDropZone,
  setDropTarget
}: TileTerminalProps): React.ReactElement {
  const projectColor = project?.color

  const handleSubTabClick = useCallback((tabId: string) => {
    onSwitchSubTab(tile.id, tabId)
    onFocusTab(tabId)
    // Trigger refit for the newly visible terminal
    setTimeout(() => window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize')), 50)
  }, [tile.id, onSwitchSubTab, onFocusTab])

  const handleSubTabClose = useCallback((e: React.MouseEvent, tabId: string) => {
    e.stopPropagation()
    e.preventDefault()
    onCloseTab(tabId)
  }, [onCloseTab])

  const handleAddTab = useCallback((e: React.MouseEvent) => {
    e.stopPropagation()
    e.preventDefault()
    const projectPath = tabs[0]?.projectPath
    if (projectPath && onAddTab) {
      onAddTab(projectPath)
    }
  }, [tabs, onAddTab])

  function handleTileDragOver(e: React.DragEvent): void {
    const isSidebarDrag = e.dataTransfer.types.includes('application/x-sidebar-project')
    if (isSidebarDrag) {
      e.preventDefault()

import { create } from 'zustand'
import { clearProjectCaches } from '../utils/lruCache'

export interface ProjectCategory {
  id: string
  name: string
  collapsed: boolean
  order: number
}

export interface Project {
  path: string
  name: string
  executable?: string
  apiPort?: number
  apiAutoStart?: boolean
  apiSessionMode?: 'existing' | 'new-keep' | 'new-close'
  apiModel?: 'default' | 'opus' | 'sonnet' | 'haiku'
  autoAcceptTools?: string[]
  permissionMode?: string
  color?: string
  ttsVoice?: string
  ttsEngine?: 'piper' | 'xtts'
  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
  categoryId?: string
  order?: number
}

export interface OpenTab {
  id: string
  projectPath: string
  sessionId?: string
  title: string
  ptyId: string
  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
}

interface WorkspaceState {
  projects: Project[]
  openTabs: OpenTab[]
  activeTabId: string | null
  categories: ProjectCategory[]

  setProjects: (projects: Project[]) => void
  addProject: (project: Project) => void
  removeProject: (path: string) => void
  updateProject: (path: string, updates: Partial<Project>) => void

  addTab: (tab: OpenTab) => void
  removeTab: (id: string) => void
  updateTab: (id: string, updates: Partial<OpenTab>) => void
  setActiveTab: (id: string) => void
  clearTabs: () => void

  setCategories: (categories: ProjectCategory[]) => void
  addCategory: (name: string) => string
  updateCategory: (id: string, updates: Partial<ProjectCategory>) => void
  removeCategory: (id: string) => void
  reorderCategories: (ids: string[]) => void
  moveProjectToCategory: (projectPath: string, categoryId: string | null) => void
  reorderProjects: (categoryId: string | null, projectPaths: string[]) => void
}

const generateCategoryId = (): string =>
  `cat-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}`

export const useWorkspaceStore = create<WorkspaceState>((set, get) => ({
  projects: [],
  openTabs: [],
  activeTabId: null,
  categories: [],

  setProjects: (projects) => set({ projects }),

  addProject: (project) => {
    const { projects } = get()
    if (!projects.find((p) => p.path === project.path)) {
      set({ projects: [...projects, project] })
    }
  },

import React, { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef, RefObject } from 'react'
import { TitleBar } from '../components/TitleBar'
import { Sidebar } from '../components/Sidebar'
import { TerminalTabs } from '../components/TerminalTabs'
import { Terminal } from '../components/terminal/Terminal'
import { TiledTerminalView } from '../components/TiledTerminalView'
import { SettingsModal } from '../components/SettingsModal'
import { MakeProjectModal } from '../components/MakeProjectModal'
import { ErrorBoundary } from '../components/ErrorBoundary'
import { FileBrowser } from '../components/mobile/FileBrowser'
import type { HostConfig } from '../hooks/useHostConnection'
import { useWorkspaceStore } from '../stores/workspace'
import { useVoice } from '../contexts/VoiceContext'
import { useModals } from '../contexts/ModalContext'
import {
  useInstallation,
  useUpdater,
  useViewState,
  useWorkspaceLoader,
  useSessionPolling,
  useApiListeners,
  useProjectHandlers
} from '../hooks'
import type { Api } from '../api'
import { InstallationPrompt } from './InstallationPrompt'
import { MobileConnectModal } from './MobileConnectModal'

export interface MainAppProps {
  api: Api
  isElectron: boolean
  onDisconnect?: () => void
}

export function MainApp({ api, isElectron, onDisconnect }: MainAppProps): React.ReactElement {
  const {
    projects,
    openTabs,
    activeTabId,
    categories,
    addProject,
    removeProject,
    updateProject,
    addTab,
    removeTab,
    updateTab,
    setActiveTab
  } = useWorkspaceStore()

  const { voiceOutputEnabled, setProjectVoice } = useVoice()
  const voiceOutputEnabledRef = useRef(voiceOutputEnabled)

  // Modal state from context
  const { settingsOpen, makeProjectOpen, openSettings, closeSettings, openMakeProject, closeMakeProject } = useModals()

  // Installation state from hook
  const {
    claudeInstalled,
    npmInstalled,
    gitBashInstalled,
    installing,
    installError,
    installMessage,
    checkInstallation,
    handleInstallNode,
    handleInstallGit,
    handleInstallClaude
  } = useInstallation()

  // Updater state from hook
  const { appVersion, updateStatus, downloadUpdate, installUpdate } = useUpdater()

  // View state from hook
  const {
    viewMode,
    tileLayout,
    lastFocusedTabId,
    sidebarWidth,
    sidebarCollapsed,
    setViewMode,
    setTileLayout,
    setLastFocusedTabId,
    setSidebarWidth,
    setSidebarCollapsed,
    toggleViewMode
  } = useViewState()

  // Workspace loader hook
  const {
    loading,
    currentTheme,
    settings,
    setCurrentTheme,
    setSettings
  } = useWorkspaceLoader({
    api,
    checkInstallation,
    setViewMode,
    setTileLayout
  })

  // Session polling hook
  useSessionPolling({ api, openTabs, updateTab })

  // API listeners hook
  useApiListeners({
    api,
    projects,
    settings,
    addTab,
    updateTab,
    setActiveTab
  })

  // Project handlers hook
  const {
    handleAddProject,
    handleAddProjectsFromParent,
    handleOpenSession,
    handleOpenSessionAtPosition,
    handleCloseTab,

1→import { useState, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { TileLayout } from '../components/TiledTerminalView'
     3→
     4→type ViewMode = 'tabs' | 'tiled'
     5→
     6→interface UseViewStateReturn {
     7→  viewMode: ViewMode
     8→  tileLayout: TileLayout[]
     9→  lastFocusedTabId: string | null
    10→  sidebarWidth: number
    11→  sidebarCollapsed: boolean
    12→  setViewMode: (mode: ViewMode) => void
    13→  setTileLayout: (layout: TileLayout[]) => void
    14→  setLastFocusedTabId: (id: string | null) => void
    15→  setSidebarWidth: (width: number) => void
    16→  setSidebarCollapsed: (collapsed: boolean) => void
    17→  toggleViewMode: () => void
    18→}
    19→
    20→export function useViewState(): UseViewStateReturn {
    21→  const [viewMode, setViewModeState] = useState<ViewMode>('tabs')
    22→  const [tileLayout, setTileLayout] = useState<TileLayout[]>([])
    23→  const [lastFocusedTabId, setLastFocusedTabId] = useState<string | null>(null)
    24→  const [sidebarWidth, setSidebarWidth] = useState(280)
    25→  const [sidebarCollapsed, setSidebarCollapsed] = useState(false)
    26→
    27→  const setViewMode = useCallback((mode: ViewMode) => {
    28→    setViewModeState(mode)
    29→    // Trigger resize events to force terminals to refit after view mode change
    30→    setTimeout(() => window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize')), 50)
    31→    setTimeout(() => window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize')), 150)
    32→    setTimeout(() => window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize')), 300)
    33→  }, [])
    34→
    35→  const toggleViewMode = useCallback(() => {
    36→    setViewMode(viewMode === 'tabs' ? 'tiled' : 'tabs')
    37→  }, [viewMode, setViewMode])
    38→
    39→  return {
    40→    viewMode,
    41→    tileLayout,
    42→    lastFocusedTabId,
    43→    sidebarWidth,
    44→    sidebarCollapsed,
    45→    setViewMode,
    46→    setTileLayout,
    47→    setLastFocusedTabId,
    48→    setSidebarWidth,
    49→    setSidebarCollapsed,
    50→    toggleViewMode
    51→  }
    52→}
